human:
POINTS:
- [test-framework/junit5/src/main/java/io/quarkus/test/junit/classloading/FacadeClassLoader.java:55] > But. An instance factory approach would still leave some functional gaps that we/I resolve by loading classes with the right classloader pre-discovery. For example, if an extension used bytecode instrumentation to add some JUnit annotations to a class, like `@Disabled` or `@ExtendWith`, swapping the classes at instantiation-time would be way too late. By that time JUnit would have already inspected the class and made evaluation decisions.

Are extensions doing this today, or is this more an abundance-of-caution thing? If the latter, I wouldn't feel badly about establishing a rule that this shall not be done.
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR rearchitects test class loading to use the runtime classloader instead of the deployment classloader, fixing multiple longstanding issues (#45349, #8446, #42000, #42006, #44320, #38987, #46383). The changes introduce a new classloading architecture with a FacadeClassLoader, modify core classloader creation in CuratedApplication, and update numerous integration tests. Key concerns include thread-safety of static classloader fields, incomplete handling of the @TestProfile on @Nested breaking change, and potential config release race conditions during test restarts.

POINTS:
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/dev/testing/JunitTestRunner.java:121] `private static QuarkusClassLoader firstDeploymentClassLoader;` is declared without `volatile`. This static mutable reference is shared across potentially concurrent test runner threads. The codebase consistently uses `volatile` for such fields (see `TestSupport.java`'s `volatile QuarkusCompiler compiler`, `QuarkusClassLoader.java`'s `volatile byte status`, and `QuarkusTestExtension.java`'s `private static volatile ScheduledExecutorService hangDetectionExecutor`). Add `volatile` to ensure memory visibility guarantees across threads.

- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/dev/testing/JunitTestRunner.java:121] The static `firstDeploymentClassLoader` field holds a strong reference to a classloader "for loading support classes." Since this is never cleared, previously closed classloaders may not be garbage collected if any loaded classes or their static fields retain references back. Consider whether this caching strategy could lead to metaspace leaks during extended dev mode sessions with multiple test runs. A WeakReference or explicit cleanup in `abort()`/test completion would mitigate this.

- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/dev/testing/JunitTestRunner.java:101] `FACADE_CLASS_LOADER_NAME = "io.quarkus.test.junit.classloading.FacadeClassLoader"` references a class not present in the base commit. This class is likely in the omitted changed files. Verify the FacadeClassLoader properly delegates class loading between deployment and runtime classloaders, and that it handles edge cases like service loader lookups and reflection-heavy frameworks (e.g., CDI, MicroProfile Config).

- [integration-tests/injectmock/src/test/java/io/quarkus/it/mockbean/WithTestProfileAndNestedTest.java:48-50] `@Nested @TestProfile(Profile.class)` is used on the inner class. The PR description states "@TestProfile on @Nested tests are not supported anymore" as a breaking change. Ensure this annotation is removed or the test is restructured. The base commit shows this pattern is still active and would fail silently or incorrectly under the new classloader model since nested classes must share a classloader with their enclosing class.

- [integration-tests/main/src/test/java/io/quarkus/it/main/QuarkusTestNestedWithTestProfileTestCase.java:39-41] Same issue as above — `@Nested @TestProfile(ModernEnglishProfile.class)` on `ModernEnglishCase` inner class. This will be broken by the new architecture. The PR should either remove the nested @TestProfile, add @Disabled with issue reference, or restructure the test (following the pattern in `QuarkusTestNested1WithCommonCaseTest.java` which uses @TestProfile at the top level).

- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/runtime/configuration/QuarkusConfigFactory.java:29-31] In `setConfig()`, there's a check-then-act pattern on the volatile `config` field that is NOT atomic: `if (QuarkusConfigFactory.config != null)` followed by `configProviderResolver.releaseConfig(...)` and `config = null`. During rapid test restarts (especially with multiple profiles creating different runtime classloaders), concurrent calls could release a config another thread just installed. Consider synchronizing this block or using atomic compare-and-swap.

- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/dev/testing/CurrentTestApplication.java:10-11] The TODO comment ("It is only needed for QuarkusMainTest, so we may be able to find a better way. For example, what about JUnit state?") is valid. Using a public static volatile field for passing CuratedApplication is fragile and bypasses proper lifecycle management. Track a follow-up issue to replace this with JUnit extension state or similar mechanism.

- [integration-tests/main/src/test/java/io/quarkus/it/main/testing/repro42006/Repro42006Test.java] The `@Disabled` annotation is being removed to claim the issue is fixed. Verify that lambda classes (like `SerializableSupplier` and `SerializableCustom`) are now correctly accessible from the runtime classloader. Lambda classes are generated by the JVM and can be tricky with classloader boundaries — ensure the FacadeClassLoader properly handles synthetic/generated class loading.

- [independent-projects/bootstrap/core/src/main/java/io/quarkus/bootstrap/app/CuratedApplication.java] The `createDeploymentClassLoader()` and `createRuntimeClassLoader()` methods have been modified. In `createRuntimeClassLoader()`, verify that `setAggregateParentResources(true)` is set consistently across all call sites. The base file shows `getOrCreateBaseRuntimeClassLoader()` does NOT set this, which could cause resource lookup inconsistencies between flat and non-flat classpath modes.

- [extensions/devservices/h2/src/main/java/io/quarkus/devservices/h2/deployment/H2DevServicesProcessor.java:66] `t.printStackTrace()` is used instead of proper logging when catching SQLException during database cleanup. This is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which uses `LOG.info()`/`LOG.debug()`. Replace with `LOG.debugf(t, "Failed to set DB_CLOSE_DELAY during H2 shutdown")`.
v5:
SUMMARY:
This PR performs a major architectural change to load test classes with the runtime classloader instead of the deployment classloader, resolving multiple classloading-related bugs (#45349, #8446, #42000, #42006, #44320, #38987, #46383). The change introduces a new FacadeClassLoader (implementation in truncated diff), refactors test extension state management for cross-classloader scenarios, and removes several @Disabled annotations on previously broken tests. Notable concerns include a deliberate H2 port leak workaround, missing error handling in a new helper method, and significant code duplication.

POINTS:
- [extensions/devservices/h2/src/main/java/io/quarkus/devservices/h2/deployment/H2DevServicesProcessor.java:93] Hardcoded `hackPendingDeferredDevServiceStart = true` completely disables H2 TCP server shutdown, causing a port leak on every dev mode restart during testing. The TODO acknowledges this ("Yes, this is a port leak"). While this is a workaround for #45785/#45786 (deferred dev services start), it should be tracked as a separate action item with an explicit "port leak" label so it's visible to users running many test iterations in dev mode.

- [test-framework/junit5/src/main/java/io/quarkus/test/junit/AbstractJvmQuarkusTestExtension.java:136] The `getCuratedApplication` method performs an unchecked cast `((QuarkusClassLoader) requiredTestClass.getClassLoader())` with no try-catch and no null-check on the result. If a test is loaded by a non-Quarkus classloader (e.g., in certain multi-module scenarios or IDE setups), this will throw an opaque ClassCastException. Contrast with `QuarkusTestExtension.getClassLoaderFromTestClass()` which provides descriptive error handling. Recommend consistent error handling here.

- [test-framework/junit5/src/main/java/io/quarkus/test/junit/AppMakerHelper.java:1-297] New helper class duplicates substantial path resolution logic already present in QuarkusMainTestExtension.getCuratedApplication() (lines 118-230 in that file). The author's TODO notes "Almost all of it?" is duplicated. This duplication increases maintenance burden — recommend extracting shared path resolution into a single utility method or making AppMakerHelper the single source of truth with QuarkusMainTestExtension delegating to it.

- [test-framework/common/src/main/java/io/quarkus/test/common/TestResourceManager.java:304] The raw cast `(Class<? extends QuarkusTestResourceLifecycleManager>) entry.clazz` is unsafe when classes cross classloader boundaries. Since QuarkusTestResourceLifecycleManager itself may be loaded by different classloaders in this new architecture, the cast could fail at runtime even if the logical types are compatible. Consider using a wrapper or interface-based approach (similar to ClassCoercingTestProfile) instead of raw casting.

- [test-framework/junit5/src/main/java/io/quarkus/test/junit/AbstractQuarkusTestWithContextExtension.java:63] The `QuarkusTestExtensionState.clone(o)` static method is called but the implementation is in the truncated portion of the diff. If this method uses serialization for cloning (as the removed `NewSerializingDeepClone` did), ensure that all fields (especially `Thread shutdownHook` and `Closeable testResourceManager`) are handled correctly — threads should typically be recreated rather than cloned, and Closeables should not be shared across classloaders.

- [test-framework/junit5/src/main/java/io/quarkus/test/junit/QuarkusTestExtension.java:164] The `TODO` comment "we should do much less of this, because it's being done upfront by the interceptor" suggests the author is aware of redundant startup work in `doJavaStart`. Verify that the FacadeClassLoader interceptor path fully handles augmentation and startup action creation so that `doJavaStart` doesn't repeat work already done.

- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/runner/bootstrap/StartupActionImpl.java:82] TODO comment "Need to do recreations in JUnitTestRunner for dev mode case" is a known gap. Ensure this doesn't cause issues in dev mode test scenarios where the runtime classloader needs to be recreated.

- [test-framework/junit5/src/main/java/io/quarkus/test/junit/ClassCoercingTestProfile.java:59] The `testResources()` method has a TODO noting it's "not safe, because testResources will be in the wrong class, so we would need to wrap them as well." This is a known limitation that could cause issues when test resources cross classloader boundaries. Recommend tracking this as a follow-up issue.

- [integration-tests/main/src/test/java/io/quarkus/it/main/QuarkusTestNestedWithTestProfileTestCase.java:42] The @Disabled annotation on `ModernEnglishCase` with a descriptive message documenting why @TestProfile on @Nested is unsupported is a good practice — ensures the regression is tracked and prevents silent removal of this test when the limitation is eventually addressed.

- [independent-projects/bootstrap/core/src/main/java/io/quarkus/bootstrap/classloading/QuarkusClassLoader.java:50-51] Adding `curatedApplication` and `startupAction` back-references to QuarkusClassLoader creates bidirectional references (QuarkusClassLoader ↔ CuratedApplication). Ensure these don't prevent garbage collection of classloaders after test completion — this could contribute to the "increased memory footprint" noted in the PR description.
